deduction_log.rule_reference: Landing Vector: Torso angle approx 10-15 degrees forward of vertical. CoM slightly leading the base of support, causing the hop. Width of Mass Audit: Slight leg separation increased moment of inertia, slightly reducing angular velocity.

special_requirements.comment: Long Hang Kip and Low Bar Kip performed.
Underswing from high bar to low bar performed.
Tuck Flyaway Dismount performed from high bar.

artistry.notes: General lack of consistent body tension and full extension, leading to a slightly less dynamic presentation. Rhythm was generally good, but could be more fluid.

coaching_summary: This routine demonstrates a good understanding of the Xcel Gold requirements and a solid foundation of basic bar skills. The athlete performs a complete routine with good rhythm overall. The primary area for improvement is achieving full extension and body tension, especially in casts, and refining landing mechanics. To progress, a relentless focus on body line and amplitude is critical.
top_3_fixes: Cast Amplitude & Body Line: Consistently drive casts to a true horizontal position (180 degrees) with a tight, hollow body and open hips. This requires focused strength and flexibility work in the shoulders and core.

Consistent Body Tension in Swings/Kips: Eliminate minor leg separation, knee bends, and hip pikes in kips and circling elements. Focus on maintaining a laser-straight, hollow-to-arch-to-hollow shape throughout all swings and transitions.

Dismount Landing Precision: Work on a more aggressive block off the bar and a tighter tuck shape to ensure full rotation. Focus on spotting the landing and driving the chest up to achieve a stuck landing with a vertical torso.
celebrations: Successfully completed all required elements for Xcel Gold.
Good overall rhythm and flow between skills.
Strong initiation of kips with effective toe beats.
